---
title: "Simulating, fitting, and forecasting"
description: "Forward-simulate synthetic data from known parameters, fit it back to check recovery, and forecast from the fit under new latent-process assumptions."
author: Epinowcast Team
output:
  bookdown::html_vignette2:
    fig_caption: yes
    code_folding: show
pkgdown:
  as_is: true
bibliography: library.bib
csl: https://raw.githubusercontent.com/citation-style-language/styles/master/apa-numeric-superscript-brackets.csl
link-citations: true
vignette: >
  %\VignetteIndexEntry{Simulating, fitting, and forecasting}
  %\VignetteEngine{knitr::rmarkdown}
  %\VignetteEncoding{UTF-8}
---



`epinowcast` exposes the same model for three tasks that are usually separate.
`enw_simulate()` forward-generates synthetic data from known parameters.
`epinowcast()` fits the model to data.
`enw_forecast()` re-drives a fit, optionally under new latent-process assumptions.
All three share the model code, so their outputs are directly comparable.


``` r
library(epinowcast)
library(data.table)
library(ggplot2)

enw_set_cache(tempdir())
options(mc.cores = 2)
```

# Simulate from known parameters

We start from a preprocessed data object that defines the dates, groups, and
maximum delay to simulate over, then forward-generate observations from a known
growth rate and a known reporting delay.
The growth rate is supplied directly; every other component is fixed through
`parameters`, which can set any model parameter.


``` r
pobs <- enw_example("preprocessed")

sims <- enw_simulate(
  pobs,
  growth_rate = 0.1,
  parameters = list(
    refp_mean_int = 1.6, refp_sd_int = 0.6, sqrt_phi = 0.4
  ),
  reference = enw_reference(~1, data = pobs),
  expectation = enw_expectation(r = ~1, data = pobs)
)
#> Running MCMC with 1 chain, with 1 thread(s) per chain...
#> 
#> Chain 1 Iteration: 1 / 1 [100%]  (Sampling) 
#> Chain 1 finished in 0.0 seconds.

simulated_nowcast <- summary(sims, type = "nowcast")
head(simulated_nowcast[, .(reference_date, mean, q5, q95)])
#>    reference_date  mean    q5   q95
#>            <IDat> <num> <num> <num>
#> 1:     2021-08-03   149   149   149
#> 2:     2021-08-04   167   167   167
#> 3:     2021-08-05   145   145   145
#> 4:     2021-08-06   150   150   150
#> 5:     2021-08-07   147   147   147
#> 6:     2021-08-08   118   118   118
```

# Fit the simulated data back

Because the simulated object carries the synthetic observations, we can fit the
same model structure back to them and check that the known parameters are
recovered.


``` r
fit <- epinowcast(
  pobs,
  reference = enw_reference(~1, data = pobs),
  expectation = enw_expectation(r = ~1, data = pobs),
  fit = enw_fit_opts(
    pp = TRUE, chains = 2, iter_warmup = 500, iter_sampling = 500,
    show_messages = FALSE, refresh = 0
  )
)

summary(fit, type = "fit", variables = c("refp_mean_int", "refp_sd_int"))
#>            variable     mean   median        sd       mad       q5      q20
#>              <char>    <num>    <num>     <num>     <num>    <num>    <num>
#> 1: refp_mean_int[1] 1.785791 1.760554 0.3963581 0.4034010 1.191109 1.436164
#> 2:   refp_sd_int[1] 3.211119 3.198605 0.3242075 0.3343987 2.690304 2.928869
#>         q80      q95     rhat ess_bulk ess_tail
#>       <num>    <num>    <num>    <num>    <num>
#> 1: 2.113717 2.505401 1.000072 770.4601 680.6977
#> 2: 3.486213 3.747598 1.001203 761.4185 580.5948
```

# Forecast from the fit

`enw_forecast()` re-runs the generated quantities using the posterior draws.
With no override the growth rate is taken per draw from the posterior, so the
forecast reproduces the fitted nowcast up to the observation-model redraw.


``` r
redriven <- enw_forecast(fit)
#> Running standalone generated quantities after 2 MCMC chains, all chains in parallel , with 1 thread(s) per chain...
#> 
#> Chain 1  Elapsed Time: 0.107 seconds (Generated Quantities) 
#> Chain 2  Elapsed Time: 0.109 seconds (Generated Quantities) 
#> Chain 1 finished in 0.0 seconds.
#> Chain 2 finished in 0.0 seconds.
#> 
#> Both chains finished successfully.
#> Mean chain execution time: 0.0 seconds.
#> Total execution time: 0.2 seconds.
```

Supplying an override replaces a model component with a new trajectory while the
other components keep their posterior uncertainty.
Here we compare a growing and a declining counterfactual growth rate.


``` r
growing <- enw_forecast(fit, overrides = list(r = 0.2))
#> Running standalone generated quantities after 2 MCMC chains, all chains in parallel , with 1 thread(s) per chain...
#> 
#> Chain 1  Elapsed Time: 0.113 seconds (Generated Quantities) 
#> Chain 2  Elapsed Time: 0.112 seconds (Generated Quantities) 
#> Chain 1 finished in 0.0 seconds.
#> Chain 2 finished in 0.0 seconds.
#> 
#> Both chains finished successfully.
#> Mean chain execution time: 0.0 seconds.
#> Total execution time: 0.2 seconds.
declining <- enw_forecast(fit, overrides = list(r = -0.2))
#> Running standalone generated quantities after 2 MCMC chains, all chains in parallel , with 1 thread(s) per chain...
#> 
#> Chain 1  Elapsed Time: 0.1 seconds (Generated Quantities) 
#> Chain 2  Elapsed Time: 0.099 seconds (Generated Quantities) 
#> Chain 1 finished in 0.0 seconds.
#> Chain 2 finished in 0.0 seconds.
#> 
#> Both chains finished successfully.
#> Mean chain execution time: 0.0 seconds.
#> Total execution time: 0.2 seconds.

scenarios <- rbindlist(list(
  posterior = summary(redriven, type = "nowcast"),
  growing = summary(growing, type = "nowcast"),
  declining = summary(declining, type = "nowcast")
), idcol = "scenario")

ggplot(scenarios) +
  aes(x = reference_date, y = mean, colour = scenario, fill = scenario) +
  geom_ribbon(aes(ymin = q5, ymax = q95), alpha = 0.2, colour = NA) +
  geom_line() +
  labs(x = "Reference date", y = "Expected final count") +
  theme_bw()
```

![plot of chunk counterfactual](figures/simulate-and-forecast-counterfactual-1.png)

# Status of forward extension

`enw_forecast()` currently re-drives the fitted window.
Projecting genuinely beyond the last fitted date (a forecast horizon driven by a
new growth rate) extends the modelled reference dates and is exposed through the
`horizon` argument; full support is in development.
Until then, extend the data with future reference dates before fitting to obtain
predictions past the latest observation.
